ocr.text: The evolving landscape of dry bulk shipping in the Asia-Pacific region necessitates a comprehensive analysis of commodity
volumes and vessel demographics. Notably, the iron ore segment continues to demonstrate significant demand fluctuations,
with a reported increase of 5% in overall volumes.
Commodity  Volume  PriceTrends
IronOre  10,000MT  SGD150  Increase5%
Coal  15,500MT  SGD120  Decrease2%
Grain  20,000MT  SGD200  Stable
Bauxite  8,000MT  SGD110  Increase3%
[HIDDEN]  12,000MT  SGD130  [HIDDEN]
TotalVolume  [HIDDEN]  AveragePrice  SGD138
Current data shows that the total volume of dry bulk commodities stands at an impressive 75,500MT, underscoring the sector's
robust activity. As we delve deeper into vessel profiles, the age distribution reveals critical insights; for instance, the age group
of 11-15 years has an average age of 12.1 years and represents a total value of SGD 540M. This demographic is pivotal in
understanding the maintenance and operational challenges faced by shipping companies.
VesselAgeProfile  ScrappingData  VesselsinService  MarketValue
AgeGroup  Count  AverageAge(Years)  ScrapRate(%)  Value(SGD)  TotalCount  TotalValue(SGD)
0-5Years  150  3.2  5  450M  [HIDDEN]  225M
11-15Years  180  [HIDDEN]  15  [HIDDEN]  70  420M
Additionally, the younger vessels, classified in the 0-5 years age group, have a total count of 50, indicating a growing trend
towards newer fleets, which can enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Conversely, the fertilizers segment remains stable,
reflecting a consistent demand despite broader market fluctuations. These metrics are vital for stakeholders aiming to navigate
the complexities of the shipping market, particularly in aligning vessel age profiles with commodity demand trends and
scrapping rates, which ultimately influence investment decisions in this dynamic industry.
